reviews.review_msg: I would highly recommend any math students to take this course as it will provide the necessary formal skills and background to succeed in the future proof-writing that occurs in many courses. Joanna is very approachable in the small class sizes. The pacing is fair, fill-in-the-blank notes provided, clear and thorough explanations. Ask questions!
Made the material simple to understand. Definitely would do a class again.
Really helped with 190. This goes without saying, but go to the office hours!

strict marker. never gives full marks on people giving their full understanding of their content, even if there is 2pages of showing work. the course is time consuming and a lot of the wording makes it confusing

I would highly recommend this course if you're planning to take proof based math courses later on. Joanna gave me a solid foundation that set me up for success with my later studies. I'll definitely look back at this course as a formative part of my degree
Super good at explaining concepts, I understood every lecture right away
Had a great experience with the course. Dr. Niezen explained things clearly (attending office hours proved super helpful - received lots of one-on-one help).

Math292 with Joanna was one of my favorite courses at SFU so far. She did a great job making it engaging, and her kind demeanor made it so nice to come to class! Introduced proofs in a way that didn't feel overwhelming for beginners. Far better than MACM101, choose this course over it if possible!
If it's at all possible, do not take this class. This is genuinely one of the worst classes I have ever taken in my life.

I cannot stress enough how incredible this course was and how amazing Dr Neizen was as a prof. If you are a math student at SFU, you should absolutely take her 292 course instead of MACM 101. She really cares if you understand the material, and the material is helping me in STAT 270, Math 242 and all my other math courses. She's awesome.
This class is really hard and time consuming, I took it online over the summer so luckily I didn't have other courses at the time. She does not give nearly enough time for exams.

Super nice person. The lectures are good (but need to go to them since there are no posted notes), the assignments are fair, and the tests and final were more than fair too. Would definitely take another class with her if I needed to.

Honestly, I don't think I've had a worse prof at SFU. She is not caring and does not provide help when students are struggling. You have to put in a ridiculous amount of work in this class. Avoid at all costs especially with Joanna
Joanna is super sweet and really cares about you getting your work in!! One of the reasons I really liked this class was because of her!

Joanna was very nice and incredibly helpful, but this class was terrible. Basically something is due every single day and the material is not easy especially if you haven't taken a math class in a while. Stay on top of everything each week and study! Also go to the QW count for participation and will be the easiest 8% you'll get!

Very good prof. Assignments can be challenging, but she did allow resubmissions which helped to learn from mistakes. Explains concepts very clearly and is willing to go back to clarify anything. Very approachable and kind, would strongly recommend!
Had an amazing time with the course!!!

Joanna is an amazing prof. You can tell she's really enthusiastic about what she teaches and it shows in her classes. Her assignments can be challenging, but they're helpful. Her tests are fair, and she's very approachable because she cares about her students. I'd highly recommend anyone to take a math course with Joanna!
Awesome professor, very enthusiastic lectures and well-explained. Makes challenging concepts fun and approachable. Very friendly and helpful. Highly recommend Joanna as a prof.

Took her course online. I have a disability and she was NOT accommodating. She made me feel stupid for not understanding, wouldn't give me clear instructions on how I could pass as I need this course for Education, and was not helpful when I told her I had a tutor. No matter how hard I studied, and even if I understood material, I still did bad.
Just suggest go to the office hours! Great prof.

Joanna cares for everyone's success and takes the time to teach us concepts. She goes over challenging concepts in depth and if someone does not understand she will go over it again. I recommend taking Math 150 with her she's a fantastic teacher.

Joanna is such a magical unicorn from my dreams. She was so lovely to teach MATH 150. However, you must work hard in this class to receive a grade in the A range because Joanna has many quizzes and three exams covering many class materials.

Joanna was a good prof, provided tons of examples and laid out clearly what the midterms and final would be like. For the exams I would say review the seminar assignments, do the practice exams, and go over some homework questions and you'll be good.
The final was tricky, but the practice tests were also tricky. I switched over sections from the other prof, and found Joanna to be easier to followup.
